replies.text: ​@taqresu5865literally a promise not to destroy all life again with a flood. God did not want to do that. Noah and his family still went out and warned people to change their ways as they built the ark. They laughed, until it rained...for the first time in man's history.

replies.author: @taqresu5865
replies.text:
​@devil8975Exactly. But the wickedness of man was evil continually. We cannot even fathom how wicked mankind had become in Noah's, or as were in Sodom and Gomorrah when Abraham asked God to spare judgement if there were even 10 righteous people within. God's Judgement is fair and absolute, and He never breaks His promises.
So while the Rainbow represents God's promise after the Flood, it still reminds me of ALL God's promises.

replies.author: @abbazaba
replies.text: In Zelda's case, I prefer live action. It makes it more rooted to reality and more believable. Animation would be have been the easy way out, less risky, If they make this format work, in my mind, it will be better than animation could be.

replies.author: @Owlicious42
replies.text: @abbazaba I can see the advantages in both approaches. I wouldn't say that one is superior to the other - they would be very different in any case. Fortunately, we live in a world where Nintendo surely has the resources to produce both, if we support this endeavor enough that their bean-counters consider doing so to be profitable...

author: @generalesdeath5836
text:
I defended her potential after episode 7. The answers we finally got about Rey in 9 should have been the answers we got in 8. The big twist that she was a descendant (or even some sort of perverse creation) of Palpatine. 
The last film then could be a movie about her denying her “destiny” and choosing to be good rather than follow the “destiny” that was designed for her. 
It could have been an interesting character arc if being “evil” and the “dark side” were in her nature due to how the Emperor “made” her, and that she had this horrible curse to overcome.
